sections.paragraph:
(once שׂוּג 2Sa 1:22) i.q. נָסַג to go away from, to depart, especially from God, followed by מִן Psa 80:19 53:4 . Part. pass. Pro 14:14, סוּג לֵב one who draws back in heart (from God), compare Psa 44:19.

Niphal נָסֹוג, fut. יִסֹּוג to draw back (prop. to be made to draw back)-(a) used of an enemy when retreating, commonly with the addition of אָחֹור. Psa 35:4, יִסֹּגוּ אָחֹור וְיַחְפְּרוּ “let them draw back and be ashamed.” Psa 40:15 70:3 129:5 Isa 42:17 50:5 Jer 46:5.-(b) followed by מֵאַחֲרֵי יְהֹוָה to draw back from Jehovah, i.q. Kal, Zep 1:6 without these words, id. Psa 44:19 78:57 .

[Hiphil, see נָסַג .]

Derived nouns, סוּג, סִיג, שִׂיג.

II.
